findings.title: Transfer-out accounting is incompatible with fee-on-transfer or deflationary underlyings

best_agent_match.title: Outgoing transfer accounting is incompatible with taxed or fee-on-transfer underlyings

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on _addReservesFresh allows anyone to add reserves
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `_addReservesFresh` is `internal`, and the external `_addReserves` entrypoint is intentionally permissionless reserve donation, not privilege escalation or fund theft.

rejected_candidates.title: Admin can manipulate initial exchange rate after market is active
rejected_candidates.reason: Overstated. `_setInitialExchangeRate` is admin-only and does not affect markets while `totalSupply > 0`; once supply is zero there are no incumbent holders to dilute. The reportable edge case is the separate zero-supply stranded-funds capture issue kept above.

rejected_candidates.title: Protocol Seize Share can be set to 100%, breaking liquidation
rejected_candidates.reason: Admin-only misconfiguration rather than a permissionless exploit. Setting the share to 100% removes liquidator incentive but does not itself create unauthorized fund loss or a code-level bypass.

rejected_candidates.title: No zero address validation for admin in initialize function
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `initialize` requires `msg.sender == admin`; in the deployed delegator flow the constructor sets `admin = msg.sender` before initialization, and there is no viable path here to initialize with `admin == address(0)`.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing check for zero reserveFactor in getExp
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `getExp` already propagates `DIVISION_BY_ZERO` via `divUInt`; it does not silently continue with a valid result.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential rounding error in exchange rate calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Non-reportable. Fixed-point truncation is expected in this accounting model and no concrete exploit path or material protocol harm was shown.
